text.title: Test Fixtures Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules every pytest fixture design MUST follow. Hard rules — operator input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Stateful fixtures (DB rows, file handles, mutable objects) MUST be function-scoped. session/module scope is reserved for truly immutable resources.
rule.rationale: Stateful + wide scope leaks state across tests. Function scope is the safe default.

rule.statement: Use `yield` for fixture cleanup, NOT request.addfinalizer(). Cleanup MUST be co-located with setup.
rule.rationale: yield-fixtures keep teardown next to setup and run cleanup even when setup partially succeeds after yield.

rule.statement: Test data MUST be requested by name through fixture parameters. Implicit autouse data creation is forbidden unless documented.
rule.rationale: Mystery Guest: data appears from nowhere; readers cannot trace where it comes from. Explicit fixture params restore traceability.

rule.statement: No single fixture creates "everything the suite needs". Split into composable factory fixtures per model.
rule.rationale: God Fixture forces every test to pay for setup it doesn't use, slowing the suite and obscuring intent.

rule.statement: When `xdist_workers > 1`, factories MUST use UUID-based unique values for unique-constrained fields. factory.Sequence is forbidden under xdist.
rule.rationale: Sequence counters start at 0 per worker → guaranteed collisions on unique constraints.

rule.statement: When `xdist_workers > 1`, each worker MUST get its own database via the worker_id fixture. Sharing one DB across workers is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Concurrent transactions from workers race on the same table; tests pass locally and fail in parallel.

rule.statement: Every autouse=True fixture MUST carry a docstring stating what cross-cutting effect it has and why it is implicit.
rule.rationale: Autouse hides side effects from readers; the docstring is the minimum compensation.

rule.statement: When `has_many_optional_fields == false` AND `domain_scenarios_named == false`, the spec MUST pick the Factory pattern.
rule.rationale: Factory is the simplest pattern; reach for it unless the model demands richer expression.

rule.statement: When `has_many_optional_fields == true`, the spec MUST pick the Builder pattern (fluent with-* methods).
rule.rationale: Builders read naturally when an object has many independent optional knobs.

rule.statement: When `domain_scenarios_named == true`, the spec MUST add an Object Mother on top of Factory/Builder with semantic methods (premium_user, banned_user).
rule.rationale: Named scenarios encode domain language; tests read intent rather than implementation.
